summary: Nine concrete tools, books, and sites surfaced from 23 qualifying HN stories.

items.name: CoMaps
items.summary: Privacy-first offline map fork with route planning and battery-friendly hiking.
items.why_included: It surfaced as the fork people are actively using in the field.
items.evidence: In the Organic Maps thread, commenters called Organic Maps their go-to nav app, pointed to CoMaps as a fork gaining features, and praised its low battery drain on hikes.

items.name: Momentum Firmware
items.source_story_title: The future of Flipper Zero development
items.summary: Feature-rich Flipper Zero firmware with asset packs, modules, and scripting.
items.why_included: HNers treated it as the unofficial firmware path people actually prefer.
items.evidence: Comments linked momentum-fw.dev, said it works well, and asked what the current go-to unofficial firmware is.

items.name: Base UI
items.source_story_title: Shadcn/UI now defaults to Base UI instead of Radix
items.summary: Unstyled React primitives for accessible design systems.
items.why_included: The thread highlighted its primitive-first API and composition model.
items.evidence: One commenter said they started using Base UI early and found the primitives very pleasant to work with.

items.name: PCBJam
items.source_story_title: Show HN: KiCad in the Browser
items.summary: KiCad in the browser with real editing and multiplayer collaboration.
items.why_included: It is a rare browser-native EDA workflow, not just a viewer.
items.evidence: Replies talked about shareable footprint and symbol libraries, teaching use cases, and noted it is the original KiCad sources adapted for the browser.

items.name: EasyEDA
items.source_story_title: Show HN: KiCad in the Browser
items.summary: Browser-based PCB design and circuit simulation with manufacturing hooks.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly preferred it for easier autorouting.
items.evidence: In the KiCad-in-browser thread, one reply said EasyEDA was easier to use, especially with autorouting.

items.name: openring-rs
items.summary: Rust webring generator for static sites and RSS feeds.
items.why_included: It gives bloggers a low-friction way to revive old-school webrings.
items.evidence: A comment linked openring-rs and said you do not need to coordinate a real ring of links to share posts.

items.name: PLAI
items.source_story_title: Introduction to Compilers and Language Design (2021)
items.summary: Free programming-languages textbook covering interpreters and semantics.
items.why_included: Commenters recommended it as the clearer compiler-textbook choice.
items.evidence: A reply said, 'I would rather recommend https://www.plai.org/,' and the site itself presents PLAI as a free online textbook.

items.name: Pandoc Lua Filters
items.summary: Pandoc reference docs for Lua-based document transforms.
items.why_included: The comments showed it remains a durable automation layer for Pandoc users.
items.evidence: One commenter said they have used Lua filters for seven years and they still run fine when updating Pandoc.

items.name: IMCDb
items.summary: Movie-car database for spotting vehicles in film and TV.
items.why_included: The thread pointed to it as the concrete reference for screen-used vehicles.
items.evidence: A commenter linked IMCDb as the Internet Movie Cars Database and pointed to it directly from the discussion.

telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 23/23 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated
